Global (All).title: Cyber Attack on Major Global Bank Exposes Customer Data
Global (All).summary: Hackers breached a major global bank's systems, exposing sensitive customer data to cyber threats.

Global (All).title: Global Protests Erupt Over New Economic Policies
Global (All).summary: Thousands of people took to the streets in major cities worldwide to protest new economic policies implemented by governments.

Global (All).title: Outbreak of Highly Contagious Disease in Asia
Global (All).summary: A highly contagious disease has broken out in Asia, causing widespread illness and economic disruption.

United States.summary: California wildfires: Governor Newsom declares state of emergency. The fires have burned over 10,000 acres, forcing evacuations and destroying structures.
Supreme Court weighs in on abortion access: The court has blocked a Texas law that banned most abortions after six weeks of pregnancy. The decision is seen as a victory for abortion rights advocates.
US inflation rate remains high: The Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose 3.2% in May, exceeding expectations and putting pressure on the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates to curb inflation.

South Korea.title: North Korea conducts ICBM test
South Korea.summary: South Korea and the US have condemned North Korea's latest intercontinental ballistic missile test, while Japan has activated its missile warning system.

South Korea.title: Protests in Seoul over labour rights
South Korea.summary: Thousands of workers have gathered in Seoul to demand better working conditions and higher wages, highlighting growing labour unrest in South Korea.

South Korea.title: South Korea and US begin joint military drill
South Korea.summary: The annual Key Resolve and Foal Eagle exercises are designed to demonstrate the military's ability to respond to potential threats from North Korea.

Japan.title: North Korea Fires Missile Over Japan
Japan.summary: On June 26, 2024, North Korea fired a ballistic missile that landed in the Pacific Ocean, off the coast of Japan, causing widespread alarm and increasing tensions in the region.

Japan.title: Protests Erupt in Tokyo Over Anti-Labor Laws
Japan.summary: On June 22, 2024, thousands of workers and labor activists gathered in Tokyo to protest against proposed anti-labor laws, which they claim will further erode workers' rights in Japan.

Japan.title: Rising Anti-Foreigner Sentiment in Japan
Japan.summary: In recent days, there has been a growing sense of unease among Japan's foreign population, particularly in major cities such as Tokyo and Osaka, following a series of incidents targeting foreign residents.
